Universe — names screened today. Aligned-Bull / Aligned-Bear — all present lenses (screen · technicals · options sentiment · dealer gamma) agree up / down. Conflicted — a strong bull and a strong bear lens on the same name. Screen hits — names passing the core contrarian screen (forward-EPS growth + consensus PT not stretched + ≥3 analysts).

03Conviction Map

Every name placed by analyst PT upside (x) against the blended four-lens conviction (y), sized by FCF yield. Top-right is cheap and confirmed; bottom-right is wide upside the tape rejects.

Color = verdictAligned-BullAligned-BearConflictedNo-Signalsize= FCF yield

04Forward-Looking Signals

Predictive / lower-precision — kept deliberately SEPARATE from the confirmation verdict above so noisy anticipation signals never dilute the clean trend reads. Options-implied expected moves with a dealer-gamma/skew direction, then the technical coiled-spring precursors that flag a move before it confirms.

Expected Moves — directional options read

How to read
Spot
Current price.
Exp. range
The ≈1σ band the options market implies price travels by the near expiry.
Move
Size of that implied move (±%).
Bias
Directional lean from dealer gamma / skew — ↑ up · ↓ down · → neutral.
Adv. rec
The expected-move engine's call — STRONG_BUY / BUY · HOLD · SELL / STRONG_SELL.
Confidence
Trust in the directional read — ●●● High · ●●○ Moderate · ●○○ Low · ○○○ Very Low.
Gamma target
Dealer magnet price is pulled toward, with source (call_wall = resistance · put_wall = support · zero_gamma = gamma-flip).
TickerSpotExp. rangeMoveBiasAdv. recConfidenceGamma target

Bias ↑ the options market is leaning bullish — more call activity / positive dealer gamma. Bias ↓ bearish lean — put-heavy positioning or negative gamma. Bias → no clear directional tilt. Adv. rec is the engine's overall call combining the bias, skew, and move size — read it like a technicals rating (STRONG_BUY strongest conviction up, STRONG_SELL strongest down, HOLD = no edge). Confidence (●●● High → ○○○ Very Low) reflects how much data backed the read — a Low-confidence STRONG_BUY is a weak signal. Gamma target is the price the dealer book magnetically pulls toward: call_wall = a ceiling of dealer resistance above spot, put_wall = a floor of dealer support below spot, zero_gamma = the level where dealer hedging flips from dampening moves to amplifying them.

Coiled-Spring Watch — technical precursors firing

How to read
Watch
Overall coil read — bull-coil (upward lean) / bear-coil (downward lean) when set; else the raw precursor tags firing — sqz squeeze · dry volume dry-up · +div / −div pending divergence · ma-tight MA compression.
Squeeze
✓ = volatility squeeze on (Bollinger/Keltner bands compressed — coiled range).
Vol-Dry
✓ = volume has dried up (participation contracting, fuel building for a break).
Pending Div
A divergence building but not yet confirmed — +div bullish · −div bearish.
RS vs SPY
Relative-strength slope vs SPY — positive outperforming · negative lagging.
Tech Target
Technical band — ↑$ upside · ↓$ downside · stop = ATR invalidation.
TickerWatchSqueezeVol-DryPending DivRS vs SPYTech Target

MOSsqz✓——-12.0%↑$22.44 · ↓$21.18 · stop $20.16
EPAMdry+div—✓+div (CMF, OBV)-28.6%↑$89.55 · ↓$83.89 · stop $79.73
PPHC+div——+div (OBV)-38.4%↑$10.65 · ↓$7.32 · stop $7.69

Bull-coil the stock is compressing with an upward lean — energy is building for a potential breakout higher. Bear-coil same compression but pointing down — potential breakdown. Neither means the move has started; it means the conditions are in place. sqz (squeeze) Bollinger Bands have tightened inside the Keltner Channel — volatility is at a low ebb and a sharp move (either direction) is historically likely soon. dry (volume dry-up) trading volume has contracted sharply — participation is falling away, which often precedes a breakout as the market decides a direction. +div (pending bull divergence) price has been making lower lows but one or more momentum indicators are making higher lows — the down-move may be running out of steam. The indicator(s) shown in brackets are which of RSI / MACD-Line / MACD-Hist / CMF / OBV is flagging the divergence. RSI and MACD are the most reliable; CMF and OBV add volume confirmation. −div (pending bear divergence) the mirror: price making higher highs but momentum fading — the up-move may be losing conviction; same indicator breakdown applies. ma-tight (MA compression) short- and long-term moving averages are converging — the trend is pausing and a new directional move is setting up. RS vs SPY how the stock's price trend is behaving relative to the S&P 500 — positive means it has been outperforming the index, negative means underperforming.

05Quality × Valuation

Return on capital (y) against EV/EBITDA percentile vs the name's own history (x). The shaded corner is the High-Quality-Buys gate — cheap to itself and high-quality.

Color = verdictAligned-BullAligned-BearConflictedNo-Signal

06Signal Matrix

The four lenses behind each actionable verdict, on the shared [-1,+1] scale — green agrees up, red disagrees.

07What Changed Overnight

The day-over-day deltas — rating flips, verdict transitions, analyst target revisions and new hits.

09Earnings Runway

Days to next earnings (x) vs conviction (y), bubble sized by the options expected move. The amber band (≤14d) is where a binary event undercuts every other lens.

Color = verdictAligned-BullAligned-BearConflictedNo-Signalsize= options expected move %
Data: Yahoo Finance via yfinance. Figures: Plotly. Conviction blends four lenses on a shared [-1,+1] scale. Not investment advice; internal screening only.
